babyDevelopment: The baby's skin is still somewhat wrinkled as fat continues to accumulate. The inner ear is fully developed, and the baby can sense its own position in space (equilibrium). Lungs are producing surfactant, which will help them inflate after birth.
bodyChanges: You might experience Braxton Hicks contractions—mild, irregular abdominal tightening. Your feet and ankles may swell slightly, especially at the end of the day. Some women notice 'pregnancy brain' symptoms intensifying.
tips: Stay hydrated and elevate your feet to manage swelling
Incorporate more potassium to help with leg cramps
Start thinking about your birth plan and nursery

do: Drink at least 2.5L of water daily
Perform gentle calf stretches before bed
Rest with your feet above heart level when possible

travelAdvisory.note: Still a good time for travel; prioritize comfort.

milestoneDescription: Baby can now sense its position in the womb as the inner ear matures.
